authors_affiliation: Akash Kirodiwal, Apoorva Srivastava, Ashutosh Dash, Ayantika Saha, Gopi Vamsi Penaganti, Sawon Pratiher, Sazedul Alam, Amit Patra, Nirmalya Ghosh, Nilanjan Banerjee; Dept. of Electrical Engineering, IIT Kharagpur, India, and CSEE, University of Maryland, Baltimore County, USA

result.evaluation_split: official hidden test set (full test data); paper also separately reports a 0.337 challenge metric score on their own held-out validation dataset

self_reported_metrics.other: Macro AUPRC = 0.072 (test), 0.060 (validation); Macro F-measure = 0.105 (validation), 0.116 (test); Macro AUROC = 0.498 (reported identically for both validation and test); fixed-threshold (0.005) challenge metric score of 0.314 vs. 0.337 with a learned per-class threshold optimizer.

model.architecture_details: 1D CNN + LSTM hybrid ('bio-toolkit'): two Conv1D layers (64 filters each, kernel size 4x1, stride 1), followed by two LSTM layers (32 units then 12 units), a Flatten layer, and a densely-connected sigmoid output layer of size 27 (one per scored class), implemented in Keras.
model.key_novelty: Instead of classifying raw multi-lead signal, extracts a single subject-wise averaged cardiac-cycle 'template' from Lead II only (R-peak aligned via the Hamilton algorithm, resampled to 400 samples, split into two halves and swapped, amplitude-normalized), reducing each recording to one compact denoised waveform fed into a small CNN-LSTM classifier.

input.leads_used: Lead II only (of the 12 available leads)
input.signal_length_window: One averaged cardiac cycle per subject, resampled to a fixed 400-sample template (not a raw time window)
input.preprocessing: FIR bandpass filter, passband 3-45 Hz, applied to Lead II; R-peak detection via the Hamilton algorithm; signal segmented between R peaks; each cardiac cycle re-sampled to 400 samples and averaged per subject; resulting template split into two equal halves and swapped; amplitude-normalized to the template's maximum absolute value.

data.training_datasets: Official PhysioNet/CinC2020 training set (43101 12-lead recordings; 42920 retained after removing samples with NaN template values); no external data reported

training.cross_validation_strategy: not reported — single split of available data into 64% training, 16% validation, 20% testing (not k-fold cross-validation); early stopping if loss did not decrease for 8 consecutive epochs (max 1000 epochs, batch size 256)

contribution.summary: Proposes a lightweight 'bio-toolkit' that reduces each 12-lead ECG to a single averaged Lead-II cardiac-cycle template and classifies it with a compact 2-layer CNN + 2-layer LSTM network with a per-class optimized decision threshold, aiming for a low-complexity model suitable for real-time use.
